{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Task 2: Predict Future Stock Prices (Short-Term)\n",
    "\n",
    "**Objective:** Use recent historical OHLCV data to predict the *next day's* closing price.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Dataset:** Historical daily OHLCV data would normally be pulled live with the `yfinance`\n",
    "library, e.g.:\n",
    "\n",
    "```python\n",
    "import yfinance as yf\n",
    "data = yf.download(\"AAPL\", period=\"2y\", interval=\"1d\")\n",
    "```\n",
    "\n",
    "> **Note on this environment:** this notebook runs in a sandbox with no internet access, so\n",
    "> `yfinance` cannot reach Yahoo's servers here. The cell below tries the real download first and,\n",
    "> only if that fails, falls back to a realistic **simulated** AAPL-like price series (geometric\n",
    "> Brownian motion with drift, seeded for reproducibility) so the rest of the pipeline \u2014 feature\n",
    "> engineering, model training, evaluation \u2014 is fully runnable end-to-end. Swap in your own\n",
    "> machine with internet access and the real `yfinance` call will be used automatically.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Skills practiced:** time-series handling, regression modeling, API-based data fetching, prediction visualization.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 1,
   "source": [
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression\n",
    "from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestRegressor\n",
    "from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split\n",
    "from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error, r2_score\n",
    "\n",
    "np.random.seed(42)\n",
    "TICKER = \"AAPL\"\n",
    "\n",
    "def fetch_real_data(ticker):\n",
    "    import yfinance as yf\n",
    "    data = yf.download(ticker, period=\"2y\", interval=\"1d\", progress=False)\n",
    "    if data is None or data.empty:\n",
    "        raise ValueError(\"empty data\")\n",
    "    data = data.reset_index()\n",
    "    return data\n",
    "\n",
    "def simulate_stock_data(ticker, n_days=504, start_price=180.0):\n",
    "    dates = pd.bdate_range(end=pd.Timestamp.today(), periods=n_days)\n",
    "    daily_returns = np.random.normal(loc=0.0005, scale=0.018, size=n_days)\n",
    "    close = start_price * np.exp(np.cumsum(daily_returns))\n",
    "    open_ = close * (1 + np.random.normal(0, 0.004, n_days))\n",
    "    high = np.maximum(open_, close) * (1 + np.abs(np.random.normal(0, 0.006, n_days)))\n",
    "    low = np.minimum(open_, close) * (1 - np.abs(np.random.normal(0, 0.006, n_days)))\n",
    "    volume = np.random.randint(40_000_000, 120_000_000, n_days)\n",
    "    df = pd.DataFrame({\n",
    "        \"Date\": dates, \"Open\": open_, \"High\": high, \"Low\": low,\n",
    "        \"Close\": close, \"Volume\": volume\n",
    "    })\n",
    "    return df\n",
    "\n",
    "try:\n",
    "    df = fetch_real_data(TICKER)\n",
    "    print(f\"Fetched real {TICKER} data via yfinance: {df.shape[0]} rows\")\n",
    "except Exception as e:\n",
    "    print(f\"yfinance unavailable in this sandbox ({e}); using simulated {TICKER}-like data instead.\")\n",
    "    df = simulate_stock_data(TICKER)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(df.head())\n",
    "print(\"Shape:\", df.shape)\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "stream",
     "name": "stdout",
     "text": [
      "yfinance unavailable in this sandbox (No module named 'yfinance'); using simulated AAPL-like data instead.\n",
      "        Date        Open        High         Low       Close     Volume\n",
      "0 2024-08-08  181.234495  182.851670  180.455017  181.707401   78636252\n",
      "1 2024-08-09  180.993034  181.928766  178.966054  181.346388   73015348\n",
      "2 2024-08-12  183.129757  185.015690  183.038909  183.564727   71263040\n",
      "3 2024-08-13  188.108689  188.984835  187.971279  188.761041  116151696\n",
      "4 2024-08-14  188.097639  190.439749  186.353413  188.061139  106469096\n",
      "Shape: (504, 6)\n"
     ]
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 1. Feature engineering \u2014 predict *next day's* close"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 2,
   "source": [
    "# Target: next day's closing price\n",
    "df['Next_Close'] = df['Close'].shift(-1)\n",
    "df = df.dropna().reset_index(drop=True)\n",
    "\n",
    "features = ['Open', 'High', 'Low', 'Volume']\n",
    "X = df[features]\n",
    "y = df['Next_Close']\n",
    "\n",
    "# Time-based split: no shuffling, so we don't leak future info into training\n",
    "split_idx = int(len(df) * 0.8)\n",
    "X_train, X_test = X.iloc[:split_idx], X.iloc[split_idx:]\n",
    "y_train, y_test = y.iloc[:split_idx], y.iloc[split_idx:]\n",
    "dates_test = df['Date'].iloc[split_idx:]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Train rows: {len(X_train)} | Test rows: {len(X_test)}\")\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "stream",
     "name": "stdout",
     "text": [
      "Train rows: 402 | Test rows: 101\n"
     ]
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 2. Train models"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 3,
   "source": [
    "lr = LinearRegression()\n",
    "lr.fit(X_train, y_train)\n",
    "pred_lr = lr.predict(X_test)\n",
    "\n",
    "rf = RandomForestRegressor(n_estimators=300, max_depth=6, random_state=42)\n",
    "rf.fit(X_train, y_train)\n",
    "pred_rf = rf.predict(X_test)\n",
    "\n",
    "def report(name, y_true, y_pred):\n",
    "    mae = mean_absolute_error(y_true, y_pred)\n",
    "    rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred))\n",
    "    r2 = r2_score(y_true, y_pred)\n",
    "    print(f\"{name:15s} | MAE: {mae:6.3f}  RMSE: {rmse:6.3f}  R2: {r2:6.4f}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "report(\"LinearReg\", y_test, pred_lr)\n",
    "report(\"RandomForest\", y_test, pred_rf)\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "stream",
     "name": "stdout",
     "text": [
      "LinearReg       | MAE:  4.136  RMSE:  5.050  R2: 0.8787\n",
      "RandomForest    | MAE:  9.352  RMSE: 13.495  R2: 0.1338\n"
     ]
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 3. Actual vs predicted closing price"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 4,
   "source": [
    "plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))\n",
    "plt.plot(dates_test.values, y_test.values, label=\"Actual Close\", linewidth=2)\n",
    "plt.plot(dates_test.values, pred_lr, label=\"Predicted (Linear Regression)\", linestyle=\"--\")\n",
    "plt.plot(dates_test.values, pred_rf, label=\"Predicted (Random Forest)\", linestyle=\"--\")\n",
    "plt.title(f\"{TICKER}: Actual vs Predicted Next-Day Close\")\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"Date\")\n",
    "plt.ylabel(\"Price ($)\")\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.xticks(rotation=45)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "display_data",
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {}
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 4. Insights & Final Results\n",
    "\n",
    "- Both models were trained on `Open`, `High`, `Low`, and `Volume` to predict the next day's `Close`.\n",
    "- Because daily closing prices are highly autocorrelated with the same day's Open/High/Low, both\n",
    "  models track the actual price closely \u2014 Linear Regression is typically competitive here since the\n",
    "  relationship is close to linear over short horizons; Random Forest can pick up mild non-linear\n",
    "  effects but is prone to slight lag on trending series.\n",
    "- **Important caveat:** short-term stock price prediction from OHLCV alone has limited real-world\n",
    "  predictive power \u2014 this task is a good exercise in the regression/time-series pipeline, not a\n",
    "  trading signal. On a machine with internet access, replace the simulated fallback with the real\n",
    "  `yfinance` pull (already wired in above) and re-run for a live ticker.\n"
   ]
  }
 ],
